calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susanna Cohen for John Schneider (Trustee).
Bruce Feder for former Counsel for Will Schneider.

Ms. Cohen stated that she is willing to submit the issue of fees paid for preparation of the accounting being included in the Trustee's fees to the Court. Ms. Cohen and Mr. Feder presented oral argument to support Mr. Marchi's fee request for services performed which fell into care coordination, case management and Trustee services even though his involvement was as a Trust Protector. Ms. Cohen also spoke of William's medical condition, expressed that her Client feels that Mr. Marchi was very helpful, stated that Mr. Marchi was there to advise the Trustee as to what could be spent as special needs, that this matter required a level of Mr. Marchi's skill, that this was a very complex medical situation, that William did not have a Conservator and that checks written by Mr. Marchi were related to William's personal bills. In addition, Ms. Cohen stated that William passed away at the end of April and that Mr. Marchi continued on until May. Mr. Feder added that Mr. Marchi's involvement was instrumental, and that services he provided were necessary and beneficial. The Amended First and Final Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Accounting For 11/10/15 - 11/30/16
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Continued To Mar-08-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susanne Cohen for John Schneider (Trustee).

Ms. Cohen requested that the accounting be closed out at the end of December 2016 stating that the Beneficiary died and that she is working with the Department of Health Services to recover the Medi-Care claim. Status on filing of the accounting is continued to March 8, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs To Attorney
Off Calendar Jul-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Trustee John Schneider.

The Court asked Mr. Feder to respond to the examiner's notes regarding attorneys' time billed for research, travel, and administrative/secretarial functions. Mr. Feder responded that the research he had to perform was far from routine, had to do with the nature of funds to be distributed to the special needs trust, which had raised serious tax and estate planning issues. He stated that he had responded to the concerns of the examiner in detail in a supplemental declaration filed yesterday. He said that the travel was necessitated by the beneficiary having experienced a stroke, making him unable to speak on the telephone. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Venue Determination, Establishment Of Trust And Appointment Of Substitute Trust Protector
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Continued To Mar-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susanna Cohen for John Schneider.

Ms. Cohen stated that she was unable to file the petition as she was ill and that she intends to file it no later than next week. The status of venue determination, establishment of trust and appointment of substitute trust protector was continued to March 8, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Venue Determination, Establishment Of Trust And Appointment Of Substitute Trust Protector
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Continued To Feb-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Will Schneider, Susanna Cohen for and with (trustee) John Schneider. Gene Marchi in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it expected to see an indication that the Special Needs Trust (SNT) was established and asked about the petition for change of venue. Mr. Feder stated that he was not aware that they had to file a petition for change of venue, that Mr. Marchi was invited to become the Trust Protector, that the Schneiders approve and the designation forms have been signed, that the accounts were established and set up into the SNT prior to Will's 65th birthday, that bond has been filed, that regarding venue he spoke with Will, that he does not think Will had strong feelings either way, that they talked about keeping the trust here for a year or so to make sure that it was running smoothly and that it is a question of which party has easier access to the courts. Ms. Cohen reported that she spoke with a colleague in Washington who is experienced with SNT's, that she is told that it would be supervised and run like a conservatorship with the same protections that we have here, that Mr. Marchi has agreed to serve, that the trustee has agreed to forward duplicate account statements to Mr. Marchi, that the trustee intends to engage Mr. Marchi for help as to how to use the funds and public benefits and that they would prefer venue in Benton County Washington with experienced counsel for that area. The Court requested that they file a petition to change venue and that it will possibly be pre-granted if there are no objections. The status of venue determination, etc., was continued to February 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing a petition for change of venue.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Bond
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Bond
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Continued To Nov-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION By Agent Under Power Of Attorney For Order To Establish Special Needs Trust
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for (petitioner) John Schneider.

The Court stated that this is a petition to establish a Special Needs Trust (SNT) with a trustee in Washington state, that issues revolve around to what extent 7.903 conditions must/should apply, the court believes they should and that the trust is being administered and ought to abide by 7.903 for the benefit and protection. Mr. Feder stated that he has no objections, that the interest of protecting the beneficiary is legitimate and that Mr. Schneider still has some progress to make. The Court stated that venue will be an issue once it appoints a Washington state trustee and asked Mr. Feder why he was wearing so many hats. Mr. Feder stated that it was a function of timing and what they had to do to pull this together, that he will need to get people lined up to meet with the Schneider's and that he will not be representing the trustee. The Court stated that it will need to set a status for filing of the bond. Mr. Feder reported that he has the bond as well as a revised proposed order, that he will address the issues with his client and knows that he will want the trust administered here, suggested that the bond amount be $620,000.00, that there is $500,000.00 in a brokerage account which will be transferred to the SNT, that there is $900,000.00 in a group of inherited IRA's which will not be transferred into the SNT but that the required yearly distribution will go into the SNT. The Court agreed to the amount of the bond. The Court granted the petition by agent under power of attorney for order to establish a special need trust (subject to review of the order) and set the following status dates:
Status of filing bond is set for November 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Status of venue determination, establishment of trust and appointment of substitute trust protector is set for January 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Status of filing an accounting for the period of November 10, 2015 through November 30, 2016 is set for Feburary 8, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
